1746	// TestExecuteBeadAgentErrorWithCommitsPreserves verifies that when the agent
1747	// runner returns an error, commits exist but merge fails, exitCode=1 and
1748	// outcome="preserved" with a non-empty preserve ref.
1749	func TestExecuteBeadAgentErrorWithCommitsPreserves(t *testing.T) {
1750		git := &fakeExecuteBeadGit{
1751			mainHeadRev: "aaaa1111",
1752			wtHeadRev:   "bbbb2222",
1753			mergeErr:    fmt.Errorf("merge conflict"),
1754		}
1755		runner := &fakeAgentRunner{err: fmt.Errorf("agent crashed"), result: nil}
1756		f := newExecuteBeadFactory(t, git, runner)
1757	
1758		res := runExecuteBead(t, f, git, "my-bead")
1759	
1760		assert.Equal(t, 1, res.ExitCode)
1761		assert.Equal(t, "preserved", res.Outcome)
1762		assert.Equal(t, agent.ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed, res.Status)
1763		assert.Equal(t, "bbbb2222", res.ResultRev)
1764		assert.NotEmpty(t, res.PreserveRef)
1765		assertPreserveRef(t, res.PreserveRef, "my-bead", "aaaa1111")
1766	}
1767	
1768	// TestExecuteBeadAgentErrorMessageInOutput verifies that when the agent runner
1769	// returns an error, the error message appears in the JSON output Error field.
1770	func TestExecuteBeadAgentErrorMessageInOutput(t *testing.T) {
1771		git := &fakeExecuteBeadGit{
1772			mainHeadRev: "aaaa1111",
1773			wtHeadRev:   "aaaa1111", // no commits made
1774		}
1775		runner := &fakeAgentRunner{err: fmt.Errorf("agent crashed with detail"), result: nil}
1776		f := newExecuteBeadFactory(t, git, runner)
1777	
1778		res := runExecuteBead(t, f, git, "my-bead")
1779	
1780		assert.Equal(t, 1, res.ExitCode)
1781		assert.Equal(t, "agent crashed with detail", res.Error)
1782	}
1783	
1784	// TestExecuteBeadHeadRevFailure verifies that when HeadRev fails after the agent
1785	// runs, the outcome is "error" and the reason contains the original error message.
1786	// This covers the path at agent_execute_bead.go lines 282-309.
1787	func TestExecuteBeadHeadRevFailure(t *testing.T) {
1788		t.Run("json output", func(t *testing.T) {
1789			git := &fakeExecuteBeadGit{
1790				mainHeadRev:  "aaaa1111",
1791				wtHeadRevErr: fmt.Errorf("disk read error"),
1792			}
1793			runner := &fakeAgentRunner{result: &agent.Result{ExitCode: 0}}
1794			f := newExecuteBeadFactory(t, git, runner)
1795	
1796			root := f.NewRootCommand()
1797			out, cmdErr := executeCommand(root, "agent", "execute-bead", "my-bead", "--json")
1798			require.Error(t, cmdErr)
1799			res := parseExecuteBeadJSON(t, out)
1800	
1801			assert.Equal(t, "error", res.Outcome)
1802			assert.Equal(t, agent.ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed, res.Status)
1803			assert.Contains(t, res.Reason, "disk read error")
1804			assert.Equal(t, 1, res.ExitCode)
1805		})
1806	
1807		t.Run("text output", func(t *testing.T) {
1808			git := &fakeExecuteBeadGit{
1809				mainHeadRev:  "aaaa1111",
1810				wtHeadRevErr: fmt.Errorf("disk read error"),
1811			}
1812			runner := &fakeAgentRunner{result: &agent.Result{ExitCode: 0}}
1813			f := newExecuteBeadFactory(t, git, runner)
1814	
1815			root := f.NewRootCommand()
1816			out, cmdErr := executeCommand(root, "agent", "execute-bead", "my-bead")
1817			require.Error(t, cmdErr)
1818	
1819			assert.Contains(t, out, "outcome: error")
1820			assert.Contains(t, out, "disk read error")
1821		})
1822	}
1823	
1824	// TestExecuteBeadCompoundErrorAgentAndHeadRevFailure verifies that when the
1825	// agent runner returns an error AND HeadRev fails on the worktree, both the
1826	// Error field (agent message) and the Reason field (rev error) are present in
1827	// the JSON output. This covers the path at agent_execute_bead.go that
1828	// previously dropped the agent error message when revErr was non-nil.
1829	func TestExecuteBeadCompoundErrorAgentAndHeadRevFailure(t *testing.T) {
1830		git := &fakeExecuteBeadGit{
1831			mainHeadRev:  "aaaa1111",
1832			wtHeadRevErr: fmt.Errorf("worktree HEAD unreadable"),
1833		}
1834		runner := &fakeAgentRunner{err: fmt.Errorf("agent exploded"), result: nil}
1835		f := newExecuteBeadFactory(t, git, runner)
1836	
1837		root := f.NewRootCommand()
1838		out, cmdErr := executeCommand(root, "agent", "execute-bead", "my-bead", "--json")
1839		require.Error(t, cmdErr)
1840		res := parseExecuteBeadJSON(t, out)
1841	
1842		assert.Equal(t, 1, res.ExitCode)
1843		assert.Equal(t, "error", res.Outcome)
1844		assert.Equal(t, agent.ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed, res.Status)
1845		assert.Equal(t, "agent exploded", res.Error,
1846			"agent error message must be preserved even when HeadRev also fails")
1847		assert.Contains(t, res.Reason, "worktree HEAD unreadable",
1848			"Reason must reflect the HeadRev failure")
1849	}

1851	// TestExecuteBeadInvalidBeadID verifies that beadIDs with characters illegal
1852	// in git ref names are rejected with a clear error before any git or agent
1853	// operations are attempted.
1854	func TestExecuteBeadInvalidBeadID(t *testing.T) {
1855		invalidIDs := []string{

1858			"bead^1",
1859			"bead:name",
1860			"bead[0]",
1861		}
1862		for _, id := range invalidIDs {

1864				git := &fakeExecuteBeadGit{mainHeadRev: "aaaa1111"}

1866				f := newExecuteBeadFactory(t, git, runner)
1867	
1868				root := f.NewRootCommand()
1869				_, cmdErr := executeCommand(root, "agent", "execute-bead", id)
1870				require.Error(t, cmdErr)
1871				assert.Contains(t, cmdErr.Error(), "invalid bead ID")
1872	
1873				// No git or agent operations should have been attempted.
1874				assert.Empty(t, git.addedWTs, "no worktree should be created for invalid bead ID")
1875			})
1876		}
1877	}

commit 001bd5e8624a3b87560f0ffd137f49de2d1ecd4f
Author: Test User <test@example.com>
Date:   Fri Apr 24 01:03:31 2026 -0400

    test(cmd): migrate TestExecuteBeadAgentErrorWithCommitsPreservesBeforeLand to real git + script harness [ddx-b9a57cb9]

diff --git a/cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead_test.go b/cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead_test.go
index 8a63e5c4..6936d497 100644
--- a/cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead_test.go
+++ b/cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead_test.go
@@ -1650,24 +1650,97 @@ func TestExecuteBeadTimeoutNoCommitsReportsExecutionFailure(t *testing.T) {
 
 // TestExecuteBeadAgentErrorWithCommitsPreservesBeforeLand verifies that a
 // non-zero agent result preserves the iteration instead of touching the target
-// branch, even if a merge would have succeeded.
+// branch, even when a merge would have succeeded cleanly. Exercises real git +
+// RealLandingGitOps via the script harness — no fakes for the components
+// under test. The script harness produces a real commit, then trips a
+// synthetic failure so the runner returns an error alongside the commit;
+// the orchestrator must preserve without attempting a merge.
 func TestExecuteBeadAgentErrorWithCommitsPreservesBeforeLand(t *testing.T) {
-	git := &fakeExecuteBeadGit{
-		mainHeadRev: "aaaa1111",
-		wtHeadRev:   "bbbb2222", // agent made commits
-		mergeErr:    nil,        // merge succeeds
+	workDir := t.TempDir()
+
+	scrubEnv := func() []string {
+		parent := os.Environ()
+		env := make([]string, 0, len(parent))
+		for _, kv := range parent {
+			if strings.HasPrefix(kv, "GIT_") {
+				continue
+			}
+			env = append(env, kv)
+		}
+		return env
+	}
+	runGit := func(args ...string) string {
+		t.Helper()
+		cmd := exec.Command("git", args...)
+		cmd.Dir = workDir
+		cmd.Env = scrubEnv()
+		out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
+		require.NoError(t, err, "git %v: %s", args, string(out))
+		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
 	}
-	runner := &fakeAgentRunner{err: fmt.Errorf("agent crashed"), result: nil}
-	f := newExecuteBeadFactory(t, git, runner)
 
-	res := runExecuteBead(t, f, git, "my-bead")
+	runGit("init", "-b", "main")
+	runGit("config", "user.email", "test@ddx.test")
+	runGit("config", "user.name", "DDx Test")
+	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(workDir, "seed.txt"), []byte("seed\n"), 0o644))
+	runGit("add", ".")
+	runGit("commit", "-m", "chore: initial seed")
+
+	seedExecuteBead(t, workDir, &bead.Bead{
+		ID:        "my-bead",
+		Title:     "Test execute-bead agent error with commits preserves before land",
+		Status:    bead.StatusOpen,
+		IssueType: bead.DefaultType,
+	})
+	runGit("add", ".ddx/beads.jsonl")
+	runGit("commit", "-m", "chore: seed bead")
+	baseSHA := runGit("rev-parse", "HEAD")
+
+	// Script harness: create a real commit in the worker worktree, then
+	// trip a synthetic failure so runScriptFn returns (result, execErr).
+	// This mimics the original fakeAgentRunner{err: ..., result: nil} case
+	// — runner errored after commits were already made. The merge would
+	// have fast-forwarded cleanly (no sibling advance), so the test proves
+	// execute-bead preserves based on the error signal alone, not on any
+	// merge-side failure.
+	directivePath := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "directives.txt")
+	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(directivePath, []byte(
+		"create-file crashfile.txt pre-crash-content\n"+
+			"commit feat: partial work before crash\n"+
+			"fail-during 2\n",
+	), 0o644))
+
+	// Real runner + real git ops: no overrides that fake the thing under test.
+	f := NewCommandFactory(workDir)
+	f.AgentRunnerOverride = agent.NewRunner(agent.Config{})
+
+	root := f.NewRootCommand()
+	out, err := executeCommand(root, "agent", "execute-bead", "my-bead", "--json",
+		"--from", baseSHA,
+		"--harness", "script", "--model", directivePath)
+	require.NoError(t, err, "execute-bead should not return an error; output: %s", out)
+	res := parseExecuteBeadJSON(t, out)
 
 	assert.Equal(t, 1, res.ExitCode)
 	assert.Equal(t, "preserved", res.Outcome)
 	assert.Equal(t, agent.ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed, res.Status)
-	assert.Equal(t, "bbbb2222", res.ResultRev)
+	assert.Equal(t, baseSHA, res.BaseRev)
+	assert.NotEmpty(t, res.ResultRev)
+	assert.NotEqual(t, baseSHA, res.ResultRev, "worker must have produced a commit before the failure")
 	assert.NotEmpty(t, res.PreserveRef)
-	assert.Equal(t, 0, git.mergeCalls)
+
+	// Hidden ref must resolve to the worker's commit, proving it was
+	// preserved rather than discarded.
+	preservedSHA := runGit("rev-parse", res.PreserveRef)
+	assert.Equal(t, res.ResultRev, preservedSHA, "preserve ref must resolve to the worker commit")
+
+	// Target branch must NOT advance — an agent error, even with commits
+	// and a clean would-have-succeeded merge, must preserve before land.
+	// (Pre-refactor equivalent: assert.Equal(t, 0, git.mergeCalls).)
+	assert.Equal(t, baseSHA, runGit("rev-parse", "refs/heads/main"),
+		"main must be unchanged when agent errors with commits")
+	mergeCommits := runGit("log", "--merges", "--format=%H", "refs/heads/main")
+	assert.Empty(t, mergeCommits, "agent-error path must not produce a merge commit on main")
 }
 
 // TestExecuteBeadAgentErrorWithCommitsPreserves verifies that when the agent
